item.title: workspace/memory/ directory created without mode 0700 — approval queue directory world-listable
item.intent: workspace/memory/ must be created with mode 0700 — the directory must not be world-listable regardless of file permissions inside it.
item.description: Two code paths create workspace/memory/ without mode 0700. (1) src/evolve/approval/queue.ts line 58 (saveQueue function: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })). (2) src/design/tools/approve-action.ts line 61 in the generated script template (mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })). BUG-090 fixed approval-queue.json file permissions to 0600, but the directory remains world-listable. Fix: (1) In queue.ts, import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js and pass { mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to mkdirSync. (2) In approve-action.ts generated template string, update the mkdirSync literal to include mode: 0o700.
steps: Read src/evolve/approval/queue.ts lines 1-20 — confirm no import from config/defaults.js exists
Add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js' to queue.ts imports
Read queue.ts line 58 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Edit queue.ts line 58: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Read src/design/tools/approve-action.ts line 61 — this is inside a template string; find mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) in the generated script

Edit approve-action.ts line 61: change the template string literal mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 }) — note: uses literal 0o700 not DIR_MODE_SECRET since this is a generated CJS script
Run npm test — confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: workspace/memory/ directory is created with mode 0700 via queue.ts saveQueue path
Generated approve-action script creates workspace/memory/ with mode 0700
approval-queue.json file permissions remain unchanged at 0600
All existing tests pass with no regressions
